1	---
2	name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker
3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false

9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended in the review phase. Dispatch one checker
11	    per `.lean` file that received prover work this iteration. Each
12	    dispatch is independent and parallel-able.
13	
14	    **You may skip me this iter when NO `.lean` file received prover
15	    work** (e.g. the prover phase was skipped, or every prover
16	    INCOMPLETE'd without committing edits). Record under `## Subagent
17	    skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:
18	    ``- lean-vs-blueprint-checker: no .lean files modified this iter
19	    (no prover edits to verify)``.
20	
21	    Do NOT skip a per-file dispatch when the prover DID commit edits

23	    failure this affordance exists to surface, not to enable. The
24	    "highly recommended" framing applies to the whole-iter decision,
25	    not to picking-and-choosing within an iter that has multiple
26	    prover-touched files.
27	  - Also dispatch on any file a code-audit subagent in your catalog
28	    flagged as suspicious, even if it received no prover work this
29	    iter.

31	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is

33	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
34	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide
35	    formalization, missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly
36	    needed), not only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate,

38	    subagent to address it.

42	---
43	
44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Lean file
83	<path/to/file.lean>
84	
85	## Blueprint chapter
86	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
87	
88	## Known issues
89	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
90	```
91	
92	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the job of the code-audit and blueprint-review subagents in the catalog; you stay focused on this one pair.
93	
94	## What you do
95	
96	1. Read your directive.
97	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
98	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
99	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
100	   - It exists.
101	   - Its signature matches the informal statement (use `archon-lean-lsp` for hover/signature info).
102	   - If the Lean has a body (proof or definition body), does it match the blueprint's prose? Naming and tactic choice can differ; the *mathematical content* should match.
103	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.
104	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:
105	   - `:= sorry` where the blueprint claims a substantive statement (placeholder).
106	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
107	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
108	   - `axiom` declarations.
109	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.
110	
111	You may use:
112	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics.
113	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to count sorries in the file.
114	
115	You may NOT modify the Lean file, the blueprint, or any other project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
116	
117	## Report format
118	
119	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
120	
121	```markdown
122	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report
123	
124	## Slug
125	<slug>
126	
127	## Iteration
128	<NNN>
129	
130	## Files audited
131	- Lean: <path>
132	- Blueprint: <path>
133	
134	## Per-declaration
135	
136	For every `\lean{...}` block in the chapter, one entry:
137	
138	### `\lean{Foo.bar}` (chapter: \thm:bar)
139	- **Lean target exists**: yes / no (if no: declaration name in the .lean that comes closest, or "none found")
140	- **Signature matches**: yes / partial / no (with one line on the mismatch)
141	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes / partial / no / N/A (if N/A: chapter doesn't have a proof body to compare against)
142	- **notes**: <free-form, one line per observation>
143	
144	### `\lean{Foo.baz}` ...
145	
146	## Red flags
147	
148	(Section appears only if there are findings.)
149	
150	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
151	- `Foo.bar` at line 42: body is `:= sorry` but blueprint claims a substantive proof.
152	- `Foo.qux` at line 78: defined as `:= True`, suspect.
153	- ...
154	
155	### Excuse-comments
156	- `Foo.lean:113`: comment "TODO: replace with real def" attached to `Foo.frob`. Blueprint claims `Foo.frob` is the actual definition, not a placeholder.
157	- ...
158	
159	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
160	- `Foo.lean:200`: `axiom` declaration `Foo.skip_step` introduced. Blueprint does not authorize this.
161	
162	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
163	
164	<list of declarations in the Lean file that no `\lean{...}` references. Most are helpers — only flag the ones whose name suggests they should be in the blueprint.>
165	
166	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
167	
168	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
169	
170	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
171	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.
172	- **Hint precision**: <one of: precise / loose / wrong>. Loose hints (the prose pins one Mathlib predicate but the `\lean{...}` doesn't, leaving the prover to guess) get flagged. Wrong hints (the `\lean{...}` names a declaration whose signature doesn't match the prose) are critical.
173	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
174	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writing subagent should land if you flagged anything above>
175	
176	## Severity summary
177	
178	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
179	
180	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
181	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
182	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
183	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
184	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
185	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
186	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
187	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
188	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
189	
190	Overall verdict: one sentence.
191	```
192	
193	The plan agent's per-file gate (see plan.md and the blueprint-review subagent's `dispatcher_notes` in the catalog) treats every must-fix-this-iter finding as blocking. If you classify a wrong signature as "major" to avoid the gate firing, you are working against the project, not for it.
194	
195	## Return value
196	
197	Your final assistant message:
198	
199	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> declarations checked, <M> red flags`
200	- The path to your full report.
201	
202	## Reminders
203	
204	- **One file pair, no global context.** Cross-file consistency belongs to the catalog's code-audit and blueprint-review subagents.
205	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
206	- **Excuse-comments are red flags, not workflow.** Treat "we use a wrong def for now" with the suspicion it deserves.
207	- **Mathematical content match, not syntactic.** The Lean proof can choose different tactics than the chapter's sketch implies, as long as the mathematical steps are the same.
208

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

11	/-!
12	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
13	
14	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
15	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
16	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
17	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
18	
19	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
20	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
21	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
22	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
23	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
24	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
25	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
26	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
27	  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
28	  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
29	  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
30	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
31	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
32	  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
33	  outside this iter's scope.
34	
35	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
36	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
37	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
38	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
39	
40	## Status (iter-177 Lane 6 file-skeleton)
41	
42	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
43	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
44	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
45	Bodies are iter-178+ work, gated on the local-cohomology / depth-≥2
46	extension lemma (Step 2 of `thm:codim_one_extension`) being supplied either
47	from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (the `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`
48	input) or from a Mathlib upstream once the local-cohomology vanishing
49	`H¹_{x}(V, 𝒪_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point lands.
50	
51	The 6 pinned declarations are:
52	
53	1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
54	   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
55	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
56	   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
57	3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
58	   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
59	4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —
60	   Milne 3.1 specialised to smooth source + complete target +
61	   codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
62	5. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
63	   (theorem, ~8 LOC) — Milne Lemma 3.3.
64	6. `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`
65	   (theorem, ~2 LOC) — definitional re-shuffle of `RationalMap.mem_domain`
66	   exposing a `PartialMap` representative whose domain contains `W.point`.
67	   The substantive Weil-divisor `order ≥ 0` reformulation is deferred (see
68	   header).
69	
70	## Note on type expressivity
71	
72	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
73	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
74	
75	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
76	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
77	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
78	  not `True`.
79	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
80	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
81	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
82	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
83	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
84	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
85	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
86	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
87	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
88	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
89	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
90	
91	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
92	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
93	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
94	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
95	
96	## Variety conventions
97	
98	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
99	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
100	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
101	
102	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
103	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
104	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
105	
106	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
107	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.
108	
109	## References
110	
111	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex` (~750 LOC,
112	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.1 (p. 16) and
113	Lemma 3.3 (p. 17); Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.6 pp. 130–131.
114	-/

123	
124	namespace Scheme
125	
126	namespace RationalMap
127	
128	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
129	
130	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
131	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
132	open subset of `X`).
133	
134	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
135	
136	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
137	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
138	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
139	
140	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
141	
142	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
143	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
144	
145	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
146	§I.3, p. 16). -/
147	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
148	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
149	
150	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
151	of an open). -/
152	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
153	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
154	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
155	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
156	
157	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
158	
159	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
160	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
161	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
162	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
163	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
164	`f.domain`.
165	
166	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
167	conclusion). -/
168	
169	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
170	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
171	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
172	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
173	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
174	
175	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
176	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
177	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
178	
179	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
180	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
181	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
182	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
183	
184	end RationalMap
185	
186	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
187	
188	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
189	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
190	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
191	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
192	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
193	
194	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
195	
196	/-! ### Stacks 00TT scaffolding (Lane M↓ iter-191 first scaffold)
197	
198	The Mathlib gap "smooth over `\bar k` ⟹ stalk is a regular local ring"
199	(Stacks tag `00TT`) is structured here as a four-stage pipeline:
200	
201	* **Stage 1** — smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk (Mathlib instance
202	  `AlgebraicGeometry.instFlatOfSmooth` + `Flat.stalkMap`).
203	* **Stage 2** — smooth at a point ⟹ exists a standard smooth presentation
204	  on an affine neighbourhood (Stacks tag `00T7`, packaged in Mathlib at
205	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`).
206	* **Stage 3** — the polynomial generators of the standard smooth
207	  presentation form a regular sequence after localising at any prime
208	  (Stacks `02JK`/`07BV`; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
209	* **Stage 4** — a Noetherian local ring whose maximal ideal is generated
210	  by a regular sequence of length equal to its Krull dimension is a
211	  regular local ring (`IsRegularLocalRing.of_regularSequence`; Stacks
212	  `00OE`/Matsumura 19.2; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
213	
214	Stages 1-2 are landed axiom-clean as named helpers below. Stages 3-4
215	remain a single scoped `sorry` inside the main theorem
216	`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` until either the cotangent /
217	sheaf-of-relative-differentials route (Mathlib upstream) lands or the
218	project-side `02JK`/`00OE` chain is built (iter-192+). -/
219	
220	/-- **Stage 1 (Stacks 00TT, smooth ⟹ flat at stalk).** For a smooth
221	morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)` with `k̄` algebraically closed,
222	the induced ring map on stalks at every point `z : X.left` is flat. This
223	is the smooth ⟹ flat instance applied through the stalk-map flatness
224	characterisation `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`.
225	
226	Axiom-clean: composes the smooth ⟹ flat instance with the stalk-map
227	flatness lemma. No Mathlib gap. -/
228	private theorem stalkMap_flat_of_smooth
229	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
230	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
231	    [Smooth X.hom]
232	    (z : X.left) :
233	    ((X.hom.stalkMap z).hom).Flat :=
234	  AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.stalkMap X.hom z
235	
236	/-- **Stage 2 (Stacks 00T7, smooth ⟹ standard smooth presentation at a
237	point).** For a smooth morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)`, every
238	point `z : X.left` admits affine open neighbourhoods `U ⊆ Spec (.of k̄)`,
239	`V ⊆ X.left` with `z ∈ V` and `V ⊆ X.hom ⁻¹' U` such that the induced
240	ring map `(X.hom.appLE U V e).hom` is `IsStandardSmooth`.
241	
242	Axiom-clean: re-exports `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`
243	(the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00T7`) at the project's
244	`Over (Spec (.of k̄))` calling convention. -/
245	private theorem exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth
246	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
247	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
248	    [Smooth X.hom]
249	    (z : X.left) :
250	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U)
251	      (V : X.left.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
252	      (_hzV : z ∈ V) (e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U),
253	      (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth :=
254	  AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth X.hom z
255	
256	/-- **Stage 3 (Stacks 00TT, scheme-to-algebra bridge for the standard smooth
257	presentation).** Iter-192 axiom-clean intermediate helper: combines Stage 2's
258	existence-of-standard-smooth-presentation output with the `RingHom`-to-`Algebra`
259	bridge `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra`, plus the affine-open stalk
260	localisation `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`. Returns the full algebra-side
261	package needed by Stages 4-5 of the regularity proof: an affine neighbourhood
262	`V ∋ z`, an `Algebra Γ(Spec _, U) Γ(X.left, V)` instance under which
263	`Γ(X.left, V)` is `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth` over `Γ(Spec _, U)`, plus the
264	`IsLocalization.AtPrime` witness identifying the stalk at `z` with the
265	localisation of `Γ(X.left, V)` at the prime ideal of `z`.
266	
267	Axiom-clean: composition of `exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth` +
268	`RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` + `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`.
269	
270	This is the iter-192 Lane M↓ HARD BAR new axiom-clean helper: it packages the
271	"smooth ⟹ stalk is localisation of a standard-smooth Γ(Spec, U)-algebra"
272	content as a standalone declaration that downstream Stages 4-5 (the genuine
273	Stacks 02JK + 00OE Mathlib-gap chain) can consume directly. -/
274	private theorem exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth
275	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
276	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
277	    [Smooth X.hom]
278	    (z : X.left) :
279	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_hU : IsAffineOpen U)
280	      (V : X.left.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
281	      (hzV : z ∈ V) (_e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
282	      (alg : Algebra Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) Γ(X.left, V)),
283	      @Algebra.IsStandardSmooth _ _ _ _ alg ∧
284	      letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
285	      IsLocalization.AtPrime
286	        (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal := by
287	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, hStdSmooth⟩ :=
288	    exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth X z
289	  refine ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.toAlgebra,
290	    hStdSmooth.toAlgebra, ?_⟩
291	  -- Localisation witness: the affine-open stalk is `Aₚ` for `p = primeIdealOf z`.
292	  letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
293	  exact hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩
294	
295	/-- **Stage 4 (Stacks 00T7(2), Kähler-differentials freeness).** Iter-192
296	axiom-clean intermediate helper: given an algebra-level
297	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S` instance (typically arising from
298	`Stage 3 = exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth`),
299	the module of Kähler differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` is `Module.Free` over `S`. The
300	basis is the `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` family witnessed by
301	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`.
302	
303	Axiom-clean: re-export of `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`
304	+ `Module.Free.of_basis`. -/
305	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
306	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
307	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S] :
308	    Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := by
309	  obtain ⟨_, _, b, _⟩ :=
310	    (Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential
311	      (R := R) (S := S)).mp ‹_›
312	  exact Module.Free.of_basis b
313	
314	/-- **Stage 5a (Stacks 02JK, freeness transports through localisation, algebra-side).**
315	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` whose Kähler
316	differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` are `S`-free (e.g.\ when `S` is `R`-standard smooth via

491	  have hInj : Function.Injective
492	      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
493	    obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=
494	      (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection
495	        (R := R) (P := Sₘ) (A := IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj).mp ‹_›
496	    refine Function.LeftInverse.injective (g := l) (fun x => ?_)

502	      (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj

506	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by

510	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) x = 0 :=
511	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
512	    exact (hExact x).mp h0

514	  exact LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨hInj, hSurjMap⟩

640	chain composing existing Mathlib pieces:

645	  `MvPolynomial ι k ↠ S` with kernel a regular sequence, the contracted
646	  maximal ideal `m' ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k` has height `#ι`. This is built
647	  axiom-clean below via induction on `Fin n` along Mathlib's
648	  `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one` + `MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv`
649	  + `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing`.
650	* **Step 3** (regular-sequence drop, Stacks 00SW / 00OW): the relations of the
651	  standard-smooth presentation form a regular sequence in the localization,
652	  consumed via `ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`. The

655	  + `IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal`

659	+ general-finite-ι transport `MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard`),
660	the Step 1 named bridge (`ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`), and
661	the Step 3 quotient-form re-export
662	(`ringKrullDim_quotient_isRegular_eq_sub_length`) so the iter-201+ closure
663	collapses to building the Jacobian-regular-sequence witness alone. -/
664	
665	/-- **Step 1 bridge (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200).** Ergonomic re-export of
666	`IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`: for a prime ideal `m ⊂ R` and
667	a localization `A = R_m`, `ringKrullDim A = m.height`. This is the boundary
668	translator inside `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` between
669	`Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` (the iter-183 CoheightBridge bridge,
670	phrased in `ringKrullDim`) and the Mathlib polynomial-ring height arithmetic
671	(phrased in `Ideal.height`).

673	Axiom-clean: 1-line re-export. -/
674	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime
675	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (m : Ideal R) [m.IsPrime]
676	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
677	    ringKrullDim A = m.height :=
678	  IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A

680	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), `Fin n` form.** For a
681	field `k` and a maximal ideal `m ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`, the height of
682	`m` equals `n`.
683	
684	This is the substantive Step 2 ingredient: combined with the standard-smooth
685	presentation surjection `MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k ↠ S`, it computes the
686	height of any contracted maximal ideal `m' = preimage(m) ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k`
687	explicitly. The closed-point case is the substantive one — every maximal ideal
688	of `MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` has height exactly `n` (independent of whether `k`
689	is algebraically closed; the Jacobson property suffices).
690	
691	Axiom-clean proof: induction on `n`. The base case `n = 0` is trivial (no
692	maximal-ideal content beyond `bot_le`). The inductive step transports `m` along
693	`MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv` to a maximal ideal `m'` of
694	`Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)`, contracts to `p = m' ∩ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`
695	(maximal by `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` since
696	`MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` is Jacobson), and applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`
697	followed by the inductive hypothesis on `p`. The upper bound follows from
698	`Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top` + `MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing`. -/
699	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field
700	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ) :
701	    ∀ (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [m.IsMaximal], (n : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height := by
702	  induction n with
703	  | zero => intro m _; exact_mod_cast bot_le
704	  | succ n ih =>
705	    intro m _
706	    let e := MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv k n
707	    let m' : Ideal (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) := Ideal.map e.toRingEquiv m
708	    haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e.toRingEquiv)
709	    let p : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k) := Ideal.comap Polynomial.C m'
710	    haveI : p.IsMaximal := Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing m'
711	    haveI : m'.LiesOver p := ⟨rfl⟩
712	    have ih' := ih p
713	    have hh : m'.height = p.height + 1 := Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one p m'
714	    have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e.toRingEquiv m).symm
715	    rw [hm_eq, hh, show ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (n : ℕ∞) + 1 from by push_cast; ring]
716	    gcongr
717	
718	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_le_natCard_of_field.{v}
719	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {ιx : Type v} [Finite ιx]
720	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) (hm : m ≠ ⊤) :
721	    m.height ≤ Nat.card ιx := by
722	  have h1 : (m.height : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (MvPolynomial ιx k) :=
723	    Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top hm
724	  rw [MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing, ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_field,
725	    zero_add] at h1
726	  exact_mod_cast h1

728	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card
729	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ)
730	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [hmax : m.IsMaximal] :
731	    m.height = n := by
732	  apply le_antisymm
733	  · have := MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_le_natCard_of_field k (ιx := Fin n) m hmax.ne_top
734	    simpa using this
735	  · exact MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field k n m

737	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), general `[Finite ι]` form.**
738	For a field `k`, a finite index type `ι`, and a maximal ideal
739	`m ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k`, the height of `m` equals `Nat.card ι`.
740	
741	Axiom-clean: transports the `Fin n` form
742	`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card` along the canonical
743	`MvPolynomial.renameEquiv` induced by `(Finite.equivFin ι)`. -/
744	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard.{v}
745	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (ιx : Type v) [Finite ιx]
746	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal] :
747	    m.height = Nat.card ιx := by
748	  classical
749	  haveI : Fintype ιx := Fintype.ofFinite ιx
750	  let σ : ιx ≃ Fin (Fintype.card ιx) := Fintype.equivFin ιx
751	  let e : MvPolynomial ιx k ≃+* MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k :=
752	    (MvPolynomial.renameEquiv k σ).toRingEquiv
753	  let m' : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k) := Ideal.map e m
754	  haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e)
755	  have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e m).symm
756	  have hcard : Nat.card ιx = Fintype.card ιx := by simp [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
757	  rw [hm_eq, hcard]
758	  exact MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card k (Fintype.card ιx) m'

775	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial.{v}
776	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ιx : Type v} [Finite ιx]
777	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal]
778	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra (MvPolynomial ιx k) A]
779	    [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
780	    ringKrullDim A = (Nat.card ιx : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
781	  rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A,
782	    MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard k ιx m]
783	  rfl

785	/-- **Step 3 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), parameterised additive form.**
786	For a Noetherian local ring `R'` with `ringKrullDim R' = a` (a natural number) and
787	a regular sequence `rs : List R'` of length `b`, the Krull dimension of the
788	quotient `R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs` plus `b` equals `a`.
789	
790	This is the parameterised version of Mathlib's
791	`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular` packaged in `WithBot ℕ∞`
792	arithmetic, with the natural-number values of dimension and length explicit. It is
793	the additive form preferred over a subtraction form because `WithBot ℕ∞` does not
794	carry a subtraction structure.
795	
796	The consumer of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` chains:
797	
798	* `R'` = the polynomial-ring localisation `R'_{m'} = (MvPolynomial ι k)_{m'}`,
799	* `hDim` (a = #ι) = `MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard` composed with
800	  the Step 1 bridge `ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`,
801	* `hReg` = the Jacobian-criterion regular-sequence witness (residual Mathlib gap;
802	  iter-201+ Lane COE),
803	* `hLen` (b = #σ) = the cardinality of the regular sequence.
804	
805	Then `ringKrullDim Sₘ = a − b = n` follows by cancellation from this lemma.

807	Axiom-clean: 3-line re-export. -/
808	private theorem ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence
809	    {R' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R'] [IsLocalRing R']
810	    (rs : List R') (hReg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs)
811	    {a b : ℕ} (hDim : ringKrullDim R' = (a : WithBot ℕ∞))
812	    (hLen : rs.length = b) :
813	    ringKrullDim (R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (b : WithBot ℕ∞) = (a : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
814	  have h := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs hReg
815	  rw [hDim, hLen] at h
816	  exact h

43	\section{Setup and motivation}
44	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
45	
46	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) throughout. By a \emph{variety} over \(\bar k\)
47	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over \(\bar k\)
48	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
49	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
50	\emph{complete} if it is proper over \(\bar k\).
51	
52	A \emph{rational map} \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) between varieties is the equivalence
53	class of a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) defined on a dense open subset
54	\(U \subseteq X\), where two pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\), \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) are equivalent
55	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\) (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map \(f\)
56	is \emph{defined at} a point \(x \in X\) if there is some representative \((U, \varphi_U)\)
57	with \(x \in U\); the set of such \(x\) is an open subset \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X\),
58	the \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\).
59	
60	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
61	\begin{quote}
62	  \emph{When does a rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) extend to a regular
63	  morphism on all of \(X\)?}
64	\end{quote}
65	Without hypotheses on \(X\) or \(Y\) the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
66	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
67	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
68	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
69	in our setting are:
70	\begin{itemize}
71	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if \(Y\) is complete (proper over \(\bar k\)), the
72	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
73	    codim-\(1\) point of a normal source \(X\) (Milne Theorem~3.1).
74	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if \(Y\) is a group variety, the difference map
75	    \(\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}\) on \(X \times X\) has a vanishing/pole divisor
76	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
77	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
78	\end{itemize}
79	For \(Y\) an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
80	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
81	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
82	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension} is Milne~3.1, and
83	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
84	\cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-\(1\) extension obstruction
85	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
86	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
87	working with the project's \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API.
88	
89	\section{The indeterminacy locus of a rational map}
90	\label{sec:indeterm_locus}
91	
92	\begin{definition}
93	
94	\leanok
95	  [Indeterminacy locus of a rational map]
96	  \label{def:indeterminacy_locus}
97	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus}
98	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], \S I.3, p.~16, the paragraph "Let \(V\) and \(W\)
99	  % be varieties over \(k\)\ldots" defining the domain of definition of a rational map
100	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
101	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi\) is said to be \emph{defined} at a point
102	  % \(v\) of \(V\) if \(v \in U\) for some \((U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi\). The set \(U_1\) of
103	  % \(v\) at which \(\varphi\) is defined is open, and there is a regular map
104	  % \(\varphi_1 \colon U_1 \to W\) such that \((U_1, \varphi_1) \in \varphi\) --- clearly,
105	  % \(U_1 = \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi} U\) and we can define \(\varphi_1\) to be
106	  % the regular map such that \(\varphi_1 | U = \varphi_U\) for all $(U, \varphi_U) \in
107	  % \varphi$."
108	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, \S I.3, p.~16.}
109	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
110	  The \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\) is the open set
111	  \[
112	    \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;:=\; \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in f} U \;\subseteq\; X,
113	  \]
114	  i.e.\ the union of the dense opens of all representatives of \(f\). The
115	  \emph{indeterminacy locus} of \(f\) is the closed complement
116	  \[
117	    Z(f) \;:=\; X \smallsetminus \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;\subseteq\; X.
118	  \]
119	  The map \(f\) is regular (i.e.\ defined on all of \(X\)) if and only if \(Z(f) = \emptyset\).
120	
121	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
122	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus} is a method on the
123	  Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap} structure pinned at
124	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap}; it returns a
125	  \texttt{Set X} (the underlying carrier of the closed complement of
126	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.domain}, which is already exposed by Mathlib). The
127	  closedness of \(Z(f)\) is a separate \texttt{IsClosed} witness, packaged either as a
128	  field of \texttt{indeterminacyLocus} or as a standalone lemma
129	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.isClosed\_indeterminacyLocus}, whichever is cleaner with
130	  Mathlib's existing API at commit \texttt{b80f227}.
131	\end{definition}
132	
133	\begin{definition}
134	
135	\leanok
136	  [Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps]
137	  \label{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}
138	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree}
139	  
140	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 --- the conclusion
141	  % "an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)"
142	  % is the standing predicate we abstract (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf,
143	  % PDF page 22).
144	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow W\) from a normal
145	  % variety \(V\) to a complete variety \(W\) is defined on an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose
146	  % complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)."
147	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 (the
148	  conclusion).}
149	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
150	  We say \(f\) is \emph{codim-1-indeterminacy-free} (or has \emph{codimension-\(\geq 2\)
151	  indeterminacy}) if every irreducible component of the closed subset \(Z(f) \subseteq X\)
152	  has codimension \(\geq 2\) in \(X\). Equivalently, no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in
153	  \(Z(f)\); equivalently, every point \(\eta \in X\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\)
154	  lies in \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\).
155	
156	  \paragraph{Why the abstraction.} Milne's Theorem~3.1 (\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}
157	  below) and Lemma~3.3 (\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} below) both produce
158	  conclusions about the codimension of \(Z(f)\). Phrasing the codim-\(\geq 2\) condition
159	  as a named predicate keeps the cited statements concise and lets the consumer
160	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} read off the combination
161	  (\(Z(f)\) has codim \(\geq 2\) from \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} \emph{and} \(Z(f)\) is
162	  empty or pure codim 1 from \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} \(\Rightarrow\) \(Z(f)\)
163	  is empty) as a single line.
164	
165	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
166	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree} is a \texttt{Prop}-valued
167	  predicate, encoded via Mathlib's \texttt{Order.coheight} on the specialisation
168	  preorder of \texttt{X.carrier} as in
169	  \cref{def:prime_divisor}: \(f\) is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if and only if for every
170	  \(\eta \in X\) with \(\texttt{Order.coheight\ }\eta = 1\), $\eta \in
171	  \texttt{f.domain}\(. The encoding matches the standing \)\mathrm{Order.coheight}$
172	  convention pinned in \cref{def:prime_divisor}, so prime divisors of \(X\) and the
173	  hypothesis ``no codim-\(1\) indeterminacy'' use the same Mathlib idiom.
174	\end{definition}
175	
176	\section{Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point}
177	\label{sec:smooth_dvr}
178	
179	The codim-\(1\) extension argument in Milne's proof of Theorem~3.1 hinges on the local
180	ring at the generic point of a prime divisor being a discrete valuation ring (so that
181	the valuative criterion of properness applies). On a normal variety this is the
182	defining property of regularity in codimension one; on a smooth variety it is automatic.
183	
184	The smooth-implies-regular-local-stalk implication itself is the Jacobian-criterion
185	direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}; we package it as a separate sub-lemma so that
186	\cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr} below reads cleanly as ``regular local of dimension
187	\(1\) is a DVR'', with the regularity prerequisite supplied by the sub-lemma.
188	
189	\subsection{Stages 5a/5b: K\"ahler-differentials localisation substrate (iter-193)}
190	\label{subsec:kaehler_localisation_substrate}
191	
192	The Jacobian-criterion direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}
193	(\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} below) factors on the Lean side
194	through a six-stage chain. Stages~1--2 (smooth \(\Rightarrow\) flat at a
195	stalk; smooth \(\Rightarrow\) admits a standard-smooth presentation
196	locally) are axiom-clean since iter-191; Stages~3--4 (the
197	scheme-to-algebra bridge for standard-smooth presentations; freeness of
198	\(\Omega_{S/R}\) for a standard-smooth algebra \(R \to S\)) are
199	axiom-clean since iter-192. The remaining Stages~5--6 reduce the
200	Stacks~00TT conclusion to a freeness plus rank computation on K\"ahler
201	differentials, transported through localisation at the prime defining
202	the stalk. Iter-193 packaged Stages~5a (transport of freeness) and 5b
203	(matching rank under localisation) as the two standalone helpers below;
204	Stage~6 (the remaining Stacks~00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula
205	plus Stacks~02JK cotangent--K\"ahler-over-a-field bridge) is the open
206	Mathlib gap, decomposed iter-198 into the three named sub-lemmas
207	\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (6.A),
208	\cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (6.B), and
209	\cref{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly} (6.C) in
210	\cref{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition} immediately following.
211	
212	\begin{lemma}
213	
214	\leanok
215	  [Stage 5a --- K\"ahler-differentials freeness transports through localisation]
216	  \label{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
217	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization}
218	  Let \(R\) and \(S\) be commutative rings equipped with an \(R\)-algebra
219	  structure on \(S\), and assume that the module of K\"ahler differentials
220	  \(\Omega_{S/R}\) is free as an \(S\)-module. Let \(M \subseteq S\) be a
221	  submonoid and let \(S_M\) be a localisation of \(S\) at \(M\), so that
222	  \(R \to S \to S_M\) is a tower of \(R\)-algebras. Then the module of
223	  K\"ahler differentials \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) is free as an
224	  \(S_M\)-module.
225	\end{lemma}
226	
227	\begin{proof}
228	  The canonical comparison map
229	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) induced by the localisation map
230	  \(S \to S_M\) realises \(\Omega_{S_M/R}\) as the localisation of the
231	  \(S\)-module \(\Omega_{S/R}\) at \(M\); i.e.\ it satisfies Mathlib's
232	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} predicate along the underlying ring
233	  localisation. This is Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, packaged in Mathlib as
234	  \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule\_map}. Freeness of a
235	  module is preserved under localisation of modules via Mathlib's
236	  \texttt{Module.free\_of\_isLocalizedModule}; combining the two yields
237	  the conclusion.
238	\end{proof}
239	
240	\begin{lemma}
241	
242	\leanok
243	  [Stage 5b --- K\"ahler-differentials rank under localisation equals
244	  the relative dimension]
245	  \label{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
246	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
247	  \uses{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
248	  With notation as in \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization},
249	  assume in addition that the \(R\)-algebra \(S\) is \emph{standard
250	  smooth of relative dimension \(n\)} (Mathlib's
251	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,\(n\)\,\(R\)\,\(S\),
252	  the algebra-side packaging of Stacks tag \texttt{00T7}(2)) and that
253	  the localisation \(S_M\) is non-trivial as a ring. Then the rank of
254	  \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) over \(S_M\) equals \(n\).
255	\end{lemma}
256	
257	\begin{proof}
258	  Mathlib's
259	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.\linebreak rank\_kaehlerDifferential}
260	  asserts \(\mathrm{rank}_S(\Omega_{S/R}) = n\) under the
261	  standard-smooth hypothesis. The localisation map
262	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) is an
263	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} along \(S \to S_M\) (Stacks tag
264	  \texttt{02JK}; cf.\ the proof of
265	  \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}), and Mathlib's
266	  \texttt{Module.lift\_rank\_of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_free} transports
267	  module rank through an \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} up to
268	  \(\mathrm{Cardinal.lift}\). Since \(n\) is a natural number both
269	  ranks equal \(n\), so
270	  \(\mathrm{rank}_{S_M}(\Omega_{S_M / R}) = n\) as required.
271	\end{proof}
272	
273	\subsection{Stage 6 sub-decomposition: 00OE + 02JK chain (iter-198)}
274	\label{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition}
275	
276	Stage 6 is the residual gap inside
277	\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}'s six-stage chain: given Stages
278	1--5b (a standard-smooth presentation of an affine chart
279	\(R \to S\) at a point \(z\), with \(\Omega_{S/R}\) free of rank \(n\)
280	and \(\Omega_{S_\mathfrak q / R}\) free of rank \(n\) on the stalk
281	\(S_\mathfrak q = \mathcal O_{X, z}\)), one must conclude that
282	\(S_\mathfrak q\) is a regular local ring. The Stacks proof of tag
283	\texttt{00TT} ultimately reduces to two ingredients which Mathlib does
284	not yet ship at commit \texttt{b80f227}: (6.A) the smooth-algebra
285	Krull-dimension formula (Stacks tag \texttt{00OE}, in the algebra
286	chapter as Lemma~\texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}),
287	and (6.B) the cotangent / K\"ahler-differential bridge over a field
288	base (Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, in the algebra chapter as
289	Lemma~\texttt{lemma-differential-seq} plus the separable-residue-field
290	refinement of Lemma~\texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}). The two
291	ingredients combine (6.C) into the regularity conclusion via
292	\(\dim S_\mathfrak q = \mathrm{rank}_{S_\mathfrak q}\Omega_{S_\mathfrak
293	q / R} = \dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\),
294	which is the cotangent-space characterisation of regularity that
295	Mathlib's \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.iff\_finrank\_cotangentSpace}
296	already exposes. The next three lemmas package the three sub-gaps as
297	named iter-199+ prover targets.
298	
299	\begin{lemma}
300	  [Stage 6.A --- Smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula (Stacks 00OE)]
301	  \label{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
302	  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
303	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
304	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field
305	  % (canonically known as tag 00OE; tag-to-label cross-check not in the
306	  % local `tags/tags` file, see iter-198 writer report note) (read from
307	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L28206--28217 in section
308	  % "Dimension theory of finitely generated $k$-algebras").
309	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $k$ be a field. Let $S$ be a finite type $k$
310	  % algebra. Let $X = \Spec(S)$. Let $\mathfrak p \subset S$ be a prime
311	  % ideal, and let $x \in X$ be the corresponding point. Then we have
312	  % $\dim_x(X) = \dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) + \text{trdeg}_k\
313	  % \kappa(\mathfrak p)$."
314	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~00OE (\(=\) \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}).}
315	  Let \(k\) be a field, let \(S\) be a finite-type \(k\)-algebra, and let
316	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\) be a prime ideal corresponding to a point
317	  \(x \in \mathrm{Spec}(S)\). Then
318	  \[
319	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)
320	    \;=\; \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q).
321	  \]
322	  In particular, if \(\mathfrak q\) is a maximal ideal (so \(x\) is a
323	  closed point) and \(k\) is algebraically closed, then
324	  \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = k\) (Nullstellensatz), so
325	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\) and
326	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)\).
327	
328	  \paragraph{Geometric application.} Applied to the iter-193 Stage~3
329	  setup --- \(k = \bar k\), \(S = \Gamma(X.\mathrm{left}, V)\) a
330	  standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra of relative dimension \(n\) (Stage
331	  3), and \(\mathfrak q\) the prime defining the point \(z \in V\) --- the
332	  formula yields \(\dim(\mathcal O_{X, z}) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n -
333	  \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(z)\). For \(z\) a closed point this is
334	  simply \(n\); for the generic point of a codim-\(1\) subvariety
335	  (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) it is \(1\).
336	\end{lemma}
337	
338	\begin{lemma}
339	  [Stage 6.B --- Cotangent \(\leftrightarrow\) K\"ahler over a field (Stacks 02JK)]
340	  \label{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
341	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue}
342	  % \lean{...} updated iter-199 review: iter-199 prover landed the
343	  % closed-point iso as a project-local axiom-clean helper (private
344	  % theorem in AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme namespace). Companion siblings:
345	  %   cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
346	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue
347	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue (closed-point bundled)
348	  \uses{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
349	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-differential-seq (canonically
350	  % known as the right-exact conormal portion of tag 02JK; the
351	  % short-exact / split refinement is the proof step inside
352	  % lemma-separable-smooth) (read from
353	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L34087--34104 and L38724--38766).
354	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In diagram (\ref{equation-functorial-omega}),
355	  % suppose that $S \to S'$ is surjective with kernel $I \subset S$, and
356	  % assume that $R' = R$. Then there is a canonical exact sequence of
357	  % $S'$-modules
358	  % $$I/I^2 \longrightarrow \Omega_{S/R} \otimes_S S' \longrightarrow
359	  % \Omega_{S'/R} \longrightarrow 0$$
360	  % The leftmost map is characterized by the rule that $f \in I$ maps
361	  % to $\text{d}f \otimes 1$."
362	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Hence we get
363	  % $$\dim_{\kappa} \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa = \dim_\kappa
364	  % \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 + \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) \geq \dim R +
365	  % \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) = \dim_{\mathfrak q} S$$
366	  % (see Lemma \ref{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field} for
367	  % the last equality) with equality if and only if $R$ is regular."
368	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~02JK (right-exact half = \texttt{lemma-differential-seq}; separable-residue refinement inside \texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}).}
369	  Let \(k\) be a field and let \(R\) be a Noetherian local
370	  \(k\)-algebra with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and residue field
371	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\). The canonical exact sequence
372	  \[
373	    \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
374	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa
375	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{\kappa / k}
376	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
377	  \]
378	  identifies the cotangent space \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) (a
379	  \(\kappa\)-vector space) with the kernel of the right map. When
380	  \(\kappa / k\) is separable (in particular when \(k\) is
381	  algebraically closed and \(\kappa = k\), so \(\Omega_{\kappa / k}
382	  = 0\)) the leftmost map is injective and the sequence is short exact
383	  --- in fact split short exact when there is a \(k\)-algebra section
384	  \(\kappa \to R\) (e.g.\ at a closed point of a \(\bar k\)-variety).
385	  In particular, over an algebraically closed field \(k = \bar k\) and
386	  at a closed point \(\mathfrak m\),
387	  \[
388	    \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa \;\simeq\; \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
389	  \]
390	  as \(\kappa\)-vector spaces; hence
391	  \(\dim_\kappa(\Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa)
392	   = \dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\).
393	\end{lemma}
394	
395	\begin{lemma}
396	  [Stage 6.C --- Assembly: smooth standard-smooth stalk is regular local]
397	  \label{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}
398	  % NOTE (iter-199): the Lean target for this 6.C assembly is the body
399	  % of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth}
400	  % itself (see \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}); the iter-198
401	  % blueprint named a planned \texttt{...\_aux} extraction that has not
402	  % been created in the Lean file. Treat the lemma below as the in-body
403	  % closure pattern that the existing helper consumes once Stages 6.A and
404	  % 6.B land, rather than as a separately-pinned Lean declaration.
405	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
406	        lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula,
407	        lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
408	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, let
409	  \(R \to S\) be a standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra presentation of
410	  relative dimension \(n\) on an affine chart \(\Spec S \subseteq X\),
411	  and let \(z \in \Spec S\) be a point corresponding to a prime
412	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\). Then the stalk \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) is a
413	  regular local ring of Krull dimension
414	  \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\). In the
415	  closed-point case (\(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\),
416	  \(\mathrm{trdeg} = 0\)) the dimension equals \(n\); in the
417	  codim-\(1\) generic-point case (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) the
418	  dimension equals \(1\).
419	\end{lemma}
420	
421	\begin{proof}
422	  Combine the three sub-lemmas:
423	  \begin{enumerate}
424	    \item By \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (Stage~5b)
425	      the localised K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q} / R}\)
426	      is free of rank \(n\) over \(S_{\mathfrak q}\).
427	    \item By \cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (Stage 6.A,
428	      Stacks 00OE) the Krull dimension of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) equals
429	      \(\dim_z \Spec S - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\),
430	      which for a standard-smooth presentation of relative dimension
431	      \(n\) equals \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\).
432	      (For the standard-smooth presentation the dimension
433	      \(\dim_z \Spec S = n\) follows from the global-complete-intersection
434	      structure: \(n\) variables modulo \(n - \dim\) equations, see
435	      Stacks \texttt{lemma-relative-global-complete-intersection-smooth}.)
436	    \item By \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (Stage 6.B,
437	      Stacks 02JK) the residue-field tensor \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}
438	      \otimes_{S_{\mathfrak q}} \kappa(\mathfrak q)\) is isomorphic to
439	      \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) as \(\kappa(\mathfrak q)\)-vector
440	      spaces. Combined with (1), \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak
441	      m / \mathfrak m^2 = n\) at the closed-point case (and in general
442	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\) by base-change
443	      of the standard-smooth presentation to the closed-point fibre).
444	    \item Compare (2) and (3): both \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) and
445	      \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) equal
446	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); the
447	      regularity criterion for Noetherian local rings (the equality
448	      \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2 = \dim R\) is the
449	      defining condition for a regular local ring, Stacks
450	      \texttt{definition-regular-local} \(=\) Mathlib
451	      \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}) then gives \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)
452	      regular. The scheme-level transfer
453	      \(S_{\mathfrak q} = \mathcal O_{X, z}\) (Stage 3
454	      \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime} on the affine chart) completes
455	      the conclusion.
456	  \end{enumerate}
457	\end{proof}
