576	\subsection{Stage 6.A iter-200 substrate: Stacks 00OE 3-step decomposition}
577	\label{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}
578	\textit{Source: \texttt{coe-stacks00oe} mathlib-analogist
579	cross-domain-inspiration recipe (iter-200, persistent
580	\texttt{analogies/coe-stacks00oe.md}); 7 axiom-clean
581	\texttt{private} substrate theorems landed in
582	\texttt{CodimOneExtension.lean} L688--L800 under
583	\texttt{namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme}.}
584	
585	The iter-200 Lane COE prover phase decomposed Stage 6.A
586	(\ref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}, Stacks 00OE) into a
587	3-step Mathlib-chain. Steps 1 and 2 landed axiom-clean as
588	project-local \texttt{private} theorems; Step 3 landed in an
589	additive parameterised form awaiting the Jacobian-regular-sequence
590	witness for the relations of the smooth-algebra presentation.
591	
592	\paragraph{Step 1 --- Krull-dim \(\Rightarrow\) height (trivial,
593	\(\sim 1\) line).} Re-export of Mathlib's
594	\(\texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height}\) under
595	the project-local name
596	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_localization\_eq\_height\_atPrime}\); reduces
597	the localised Krull-dim goal to a height-of-maximal-ideal goal.
598	
599	\paragraph{Step 2 --- Polynomial-ring maximal-ideal height
600	(\(\sim 60\) LOC).} For \(\texttt{MvPolynomial}\,(\texttt{Fin}\,n)\,k\)
601	with \(k\) a field, every maximal ideal has height \emph{exactly}
602	\(n\). Lower bound:
603	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_ge\_card\_of\_field}\),
604	induction on \texttt{Fin n} via \(\texttt{MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv}\)
605	+ Mathlib's \(\texttt{Polynomial.height\_eq\_height\_add\_one}\) plus
606	the Jacobson contraction
607	\(\texttt{Polynomial.isMaximal\_comap\_C\_of\_isJacobsonRing}\). Upper
608	bound:
609	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_le\_natCard\_of\_field}\)
610	via \(\texttt{Ideal.height\_le\_ringKrullDim\_of\_ne\_top}\) +
611	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim\_of\_isNoetherianRing}\) +
612	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_eq\_zero\_of\_field}\). Combined: the
613	\texttt{Fin n}-form
614	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_eq\_card}\) and the
615	general \texttt{[Finite ι]}-form
616	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_eq\_natCard}\) via
617	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.renameEquiv}\) +
618	\(\texttt{RingEquiv.height\_map}\). Capstone packaging the
619	Steps 1+2 composition for any localisation at a maximal ideal:
620	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_localization\_atMaximal\_MvPolynomial}\).
621	
622	\paragraph{Step 3 --- Drop by regular-sequence length (additive form
623	in hand, witness pending).} Step 3 generally:
624	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim}\,(R'_{m'} / (f_j)_{j \in \sigma}) +
625	 |\sigma| = \texttt{ringKrullDim}\,R'_{m'}\). The additive form is
626	landed as
627	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_add\_eq\_of\_regular\_sequence}\)
628	via Mathlib's
629	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_add\_length\_eq\_ringKrullDim\_of\_isRegular}\),
630	parameterised over a hypothesis
631	\(\texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular}\,R'_{m'}\,\texttt{rs}\)
632	that is the iter-201+ residual substrate obligation. Note:
633	\(\texttt{WithBot}\,\mathbb N_\infty\) carries no \texttt{HSub}
634	instance, so the additive form is the natural API; a subtraction
635	form is not available.

637	\paragraph{Iter-201+ Jacobian-regular-sequence witness recipe.} The
638	residual substrate of Stage 6.A is the Jacobian-criterion
639	regular-sequence witness for the relations of an
640	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation}\).
641	
642	\textbf{API choice} (per iter-201 \texttt{progress-critic route201}
643	flag): the project-local target predicate is Mathlib's
644	\(\texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular}\), \emph{not}
645	\(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\). Reason: the iter-200 substrate
646	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_add\_eq\_of\_regular\_sequence}\)
647	(\texttt{CodimOneExtension.lean} L807) consumes the strong predicate
648	\(\texttt{IsRegular}\) (via Mathlib's
649	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_add\_length\_eq\_ringKrullDim\_of\_isRegular}\));
650	the localised stalk \(R'_{m'}\) is local, so by Mathlib's
651	\(\texttt{IsLocalRing.isRegular\_iff\_isWeaklyRegular\_of\_subset\_maximalIdeal}\)
652	the two predicates are equivalent under the standing hypothesis that
653	the relations lie in the maximal ideal --- which holds because
654	the Jacobian-criterion conclusion is precisely that the relations
655	generate (a subset of) the maximal ideal at the closed point. The
656	recipe builds \(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\) first (it is the
657	naturally-arising predicate from the Koszul-complex argument) and
658	then promotes to \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) via the bridge lemma; the
659	final consumer call uses the \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) form.

987	/-- **Lane COE Step B.a (iter-202).** Extract the explicit
988	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` underlying an
989	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S` instance, together with the

995	private theorem exists_submersivePresentation_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
996	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
997	    {n : ℕ} [h : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S] :
998	    ∃ (ix sx : Type), ∃ (_ : Finite sx) (_ : Finite ix),
999	      ∃ P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx, P.dimension = n :=
1000	  h.out
1001	
1002	/-- **Lane COE Step B.b (iter-202).** The affine-open stalk localisation: for an
1003	affine open `V ∋ z` of a scheme `X`, the stalk `X.presheaf.stalk z` is the
1004	localisation of the section ring `Γ(X, V)` at the prime ideal `primeIdealOf z`.
1005	Re-export of `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`, decoupled here from
1006	standard-smoothness so downstream regularity computations (Step B.d) can name the
1007	`IsLocalization.AtPrime` instance without re-deriving the Stage-3 algebra package.
1008	Axiom-clean: 1-line re-export. -/
1009	theorem isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen
1010	    {X : Scheme.{u}} {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (z : X) (hzV : z ∈ V) :
1011	    letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
1012	    IsLocalization.AtPrime (X.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal :=
1013	  hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩
1014	
1015	/-- **Lane COE Step B.c helper (iter-202).** On a scheme whose underlying carrier
1016	is a subsingleton (the topological space has at most one point — e.g.\
1017	`Spec (.of k̄)` for a field `k̄`, which has a unique point by
1018	`PrimeSpectrum.instUnique`), every nonempty open subset equals `⊤`. Axiom-clean:
1019	the unique-point argument via `Subsingleton.elim`. -/
1020	private lemma open_eq_top_of_subsingleton {X : Scheme.{u}} [Subsingleton X]
1021	    (U : X.Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) : U = ⊤ := by
1022	  obtain ⟨⟨x, hx⟩⟩ := hU
1023	  ext y
1024	  simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_top, Set.mem_univ, iff_true]
1025	  rwa [Subsingleton.elim y x]
1026	
1027	/-- **Lane COE Step B.c (iter-202).** The `Γ(Spec (.of k̄), U) = k̄` bridge: for a
1028	field `k̄` and any nonempty open `U` of `Spec (.of k̄)`, the section ring over `U`
1029	is ring-isomorphic to `k̄`. Since `Spec (.of k̄)` has a subsingleton carrier
1030	(`PrimeSpectrum.instUnique`), `U = ⊤` and the sections collapse to the global
1031	sections `Γ(Spec (.of k̄), ⊤) ≅ k̄` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`). This supplies the
1032	algebraically-closed base ring of the standard-smooth presentation
1033	(`R = Γ(Spec (.of k̄), U)`) with a clean `k̄`-identification — the geometric input
1034	to the closed-point residue-field bridge `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`.
1035	Axiom-clean: `subst` of `open_eq_top_of_subsingleton` + `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
1036	noncomputable def gammaSpecField_ringEquiv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
1037	    (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) :
1038	    Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) ≃+* kbar := by
1039	  have h : U = ⊤ := open_eq_top_of_subsingleton U hU
1040	  subst h
1041	  exact (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv

1043	/-! ## §3.C. Project-local Mathlib supplement — Matsumura regular-sequence bridge (iter-203, Step A1)
1044	
1045	Lane COE Step A1 substrate (Matsumura *Commutative Ring Theory* Thm 14.2 /
1046	Stacks 00NQ). The goal of this section is the criterion: on a regular local
1047	Noetherian ring `(A, 𝔪)`, a finite sequence `f₁,…,f_c ∈ 𝔪` whose images in the
1048	cotangent space `𝔪/𝔪²` are `κ`-linearly independent forms a
1049	`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular` sequence.
1050	
1051	The induction is on the length `c`, peeling the head `f₁`. The mathematical
1052	peeling step uses `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons'`, whose tail lives over
1053	the *module* quotient `QuotSMulTop f₁ A = A ⧸ (f₁ • ⊤)`. The two bridges below
1054	convert that module-quotient bookkeeping into the *ring* quotient
1055	`A ⧸ Ideal.span {f₁}` (over which the induction hypothesis is naturally
1056	phrased): `quotSMulTop_quotientRing_linearEquiv` is the canonical
1057	`(A ⧸ span{f₁})`-linear identification `QuotSMulTop f₁ A ≃ₗ A ⧸ span{f₁}`, and
1058	`isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring` is the resulting clean cons rule.
1059	
1060	These are project-local because Mathlib ships only the `QuotSMulTop`-flavoured
1061	`IsRegular.cons'`; the ring-quotient repackaging is what makes a downstream
1062	ring-theoretic induction (the Matsumura criterion) ergonomic. -/
1063	
1064	/-- **Step A1 bridge (iter-203).** The canonical `(A ⧸ span{r})`-linear
1065	equivalence between the module quotient `QuotSMulTop r A = A ⧸ (r • ⊤)` and the
1066	ring quotient `A ⧸ span{r}`. Built by composing Mathlib's
1067	`QuotSMulTop.equivQuotTensor` (`QuotSMulTop r A ≃ₗ[A] (A⧸span{r}) ⊗[A] A`) with
1068	`TensorProduct.rid` and then promoting the resulting `A`-linear equivalence to
1069	an `(A⧸span{r})`-linear one via `LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfSurjective` along the
1070	surjective quotient map. Axiom-clean. -/
1071	private noncomputable def quotSMulTop_quotientRing_linearEquiv
1072	    {A : Type u} [CommRing A] (r : A) :
1073	    QuotSMulTop r A ≃ₗ[A ⧸ Ideal.span {r}] (A ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) :=
1074	  LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfSurjective Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1075	    ((QuotSMulTop.equivQuotTensor r A) ≪≫ₗ (TensorProduct.rid A (A ⧸ Ideal.span {r})))
1076	
1077	/-- **Step A1 bridge (iter-203).** Ring-quotient cons rule for regular
1078	sequences: given `IsSMulRegular A r` and a regular sequence over the ring
1079	quotient `A ⧸ span{r}` on the images of `rs`, the list `r :: rs` is a regular
1080	sequence over `A`. This is the ergonomic ring-quotient form of Mathlib's
1081	`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons'` (whose tail lives over the module quotient
1082	`QuotSMulTop r A`), transported across `quotSMulTop_quotientRing_linearEquiv` via
1083	`LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`. Axiom-clean. -/
1084	private theorem isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring
1085	    {A : Type u} [CommRing A] {r : A} {rs : List A}
1086	    (h1 : IsSMulRegular A r)
1087	    (h2 : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular (A ⧸ Ideal.span {r})
1088	            (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) :
1089	    RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular A (r :: rs) := by
1090	  apply RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons' h1
1091	  exact ((quotSMulTop_quotientRing_linearEquiv r).symm.isRegular_congr _).mp h2

1096	/-- **Step A1 cotangent linear-independence descent (iter-203).** The inductive
1097	descent step of the Matsumura criterion: if the cotangent classes of a sequence
1098	`f₀, f₁, …, f_m ∈ 𝔪` are `κ(A)`-linearly independent, then the cotangent classes
1099	of the *tail* `f₁, …, f_m`, pushed into the quotient `A' = A ⧸ span{f₀}`, are
1100	`κ(A')`-linearly independent in `𝔪'/𝔪'²`.
1101	
1102	This is the genuine mathematical content of the descent (the part the iter-203
1103	blueprint recipe flagged as a `LinearIndependent.map`/`.image` step). The proof
1104	constructs the `A`-linear cotangent map `π : 𝔪.Cotangent →ₗ[A] 𝔪'.Cotangent`
1105	(Mathlib's `Ideal.mapCotangent` for the quotient algebra `A → A'`), shows it is
1106	surjective with kernel `A ∙ (𝔪.toCotangent f₀)` (via
1107	`Ideal.mapCotangent_ker_of_surjective`, using `span{f₀} ⊓ 𝔪 = span{f₀}`), and
1108	then runs the kernel-disjointness/linear-independence argument by hand through
1109	`Fintype.linearIndependent_iff`: a `κ(A')`-relation on the tail images lifts to
1110	an `A`-relation `∑ aᵢ • π(vᵢ₊₁) = 0`, hence `∑ aᵢ • vᵢ₊₁ ∈ ker π = A ∙ v₀`, so
1111	reducing modulo `𝔪` and applying the full `κ(A)`-independence forces every
1112	`residue (aᵢ) = 0`, whence every original `κ(A')`-coefficient vanishes (the
1113	residue fields are matched through `algebraMap A A'`, no explicit residue-field
1114	isomorphism is needed).

1116	Project-local because Mathlib ships `Ideal.mapCotangent` and its kernel/surjectivity
1117	lemmas but not this regular-local descent packaging. Axiom-clean. -/
1118	private theorem matsumura_descent_cotangent
1119	    {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [IsLocalRing A]
1120	    (m : ℕ) (rs : Fin (m + 1) → A) (hmem : ∀ i, rs i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A)
1121	    [Nontrivial (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A))] [IsLocalRing (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A))]
1122	    (hlin : LinearIndependent (IsLocalRing.ResidueField A)
1123	       (fun i => (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).toCotangent ⟨rs i, hmem i⟩))
1124	    (hg'mem : ∀ i : Fin m, (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) (rs i.succ))
1125	       ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A))) :
1126	    LinearIndependent (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
1127	      (fun i : Fin m => (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A))).toCotangent
1128	        ⟨Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) (rs i.succ), hg'mem i⟩) := by
1129	  set x := rs 0 with hxdef
1130	  have hxmem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A := hmem 0
1131	  set B := A ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set A) with hB
1132	  have hsurj : Function.Surjective (algebraMap A B) := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1133	  have halg : algebraMap A B = Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({x} : Set A)) :=
1134	    Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq _
1135	  have hkerm : RingHom.ker (algebraMap A B) = Ideal.span ({x} : Set A) := by
1136	    rw [halg]; exact Ideal.mk_ker
1137	  have hcomap : Ideal.comap (algebraMap A B) (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal B)
1138	      = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A :=
1139	    (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff A).mp (Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ hsurj)
1140	  have heq : Ideal.comap (algebraMap A B) (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal B)
1141	      = RingHom.ker (algebraMap A B) ⊔ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A := by
1142	    rw [hcomap, hkerm, sup_eq_right.mpr]; rwa [Ideal.span_le, Set.singleton_subset_iff]
1143	  have hle : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A
1144	      ≤ Ideal.comap (Algebra.ofId A B) (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal B) := hcomap.ge

1215	/-- **Step A1 — Matsumura's regular-sequence criterion (iter-203, Lane COE).**
1216	On a regular local Noetherian ring `(A, 𝔪)`, a finite sequence `f₁, …, f_c ∈ 𝔪`
1217	whose images in the cotangent space `𝔪/𝔪²` are `κ(A)`-linearly independent forms
1218	a `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular` sequence.
1219	
1220	This is Matsumura, *Commutative Ring Theory*, Thm 14.2 (cf. Stacks tag `00NQ`):
1221	linearly independent elements of `𝔪/𝔪²` in a regular local ring form a regular
1222	sequence (indeed they extend to a regular system of parameters). The proof is
1223	the iter-203 blueprint recipe `\subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200`, Step A1:
1224	induction on the length `c` (here `n`), peeling the head `f₁` via
1225	`isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring`. At each step the head is a non-zero-divisor
1226	(`A` is a domain by `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal`, and
1227	`f₁ ≠ 0` since `f₁ ∉ 𝔪²`), the quotient `A ⧸ span{f₁}` is again regular local
1228	(`RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq`),
1229	and the tail's cotangent classes stay `κ`-linearly independent in the quotient
1230	(`matsumura_descent_cotangent`). The two
1231	`RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.*` inputs were promoted to public in iter-202.
1232	
1233	Project-local because Mathlib at commit `b80f227` ships neither this criterion
1234	nor the regular-local⟹domain / regular-local-quotient facts it consumes.
1235	Axiom-clean. -/
1236	private theorem matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent
1237	    {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [IsLocalRing A] [IsNoetherianRing A] [IsRegularLocalRing A]
1238	    (n : ℕ) (rs : Fin n → A) (hrs_mem : ∀ i, rs i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A)
1239	    (h_lin : LinearIndependent (IsLocalRing.ResidueField A)
1240	               (fun i => (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).toCotangent ⟨rs i, hrs_mem i⟩)) :
1241	    RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular A (List.ofFn rs) := by
1242	  suffices H : ∀ (n : ℕ) (A : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsLocalRing A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1243	      [IsRegularLocalRing A] (rs : Fin n → A)
1244	      (hrs_mem : ∀ i, rs i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A),
1245	      LinearIndependent (IsLocalRing.ResidueField A)
1246	        (fun i => (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).toCotangent ⟨rs i, hrs_mem i⟩) →
1247	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular A (List.ofFn rs) from H n A rs hrs_mem h_lin
1248	  clear h_lin hrs_mem rs n
1249	  intro n
1250	  induction n with
1251	  | zero =>
1252	    intro A _ _ _ _ rs _ _
1253	    rw [List.ofFn_zero]; exact RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.nil A A
1254	  | succ m ih =>
1255	    intro A _ _ _ _ rs hmem hlin
1256	    have hxmem : rs 0 ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A := hmem 0
1257	    have hxne0 : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).toCotangent ⟨rs 0, hxmem⟩ ≠ 0 := hlin.ne_zero 0
1258	    have hxnotsq : rs 0 ∉ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A ^ 2 := fun hsq =>
1259	      hxne0 ((Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero _ ⟨rs 0, hxmem⟩).mpr hsq)
1260	    have hspan_pos : ∃ k, (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).spanFinrank = k + 1 := by
1261	      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos ((IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).spanFinrank) with h0 | hpos
1262	      · exfalso
1263	        have hfg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).FG := Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
1264	        have hbot : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A = ⊥ :=
1265	          (Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot hfg).mp h0
1266	        apply hxnotsq
1267	        have hb : rs 0 ∈ (⊥ : Ideal A) := hbot ▸ hxmem
1268	        rw [Submodule.mem_bot] at hb; rw [hb]; exact Ideal.zero_mem _
1269	      · exact ⟨(IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).spanFinrank - 1, by omega⟩
1270	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := hspan_pos
1271	    obtain ⟨hNT, hLR, hRLR, _hdim_quot⟩ :=
1272	      RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq
1273	        hk (rs 0) hxmem hxnotsq
1274	    haveI : Nontrivial (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) := hNT
1275	    haveI : IsLocalRing (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) := hLR
1276	    haveI : IsRegularLocalRing (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) := hRLR
1277	    haveI : IsNoetherianRing (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) := inferInstance
1278	    have hg_mem : ∀ i : Fin m, (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) (rs i.succ))
1279	        ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) := by
1280	      intro i
1281	      have hmax : (Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
1282	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))).IsMaximal :=
1283	        Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1284	      have hc : Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
1285	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
1286	          = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A := (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff A).mp hmax
1287	      have hin : rs i.succ ∈ Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
1288	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A))) := by
1289	        rw [hc]; exact hmem i.succ
1290	      exact Ideal.mem_comap.mp hin
1291	    have hlin' := matsumura_descent_cotangent m rs hmem hlin hg_mem
1292	    have hih := ih (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A))
1293	      (fun i => Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) (rs i.succ)) hg_mem hlin'
1294	    haveI : IsDomain A := RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal A
1295	    have hxne0' : rs 0 ≠ 0 := fun h => hxnotsq (by rw [h]; exact Ideal.zero_mem _)
1296	    have h1 : IsSMulRegular A (rs 0) := IsSMulRegular.of_ne_zero hxne0'
1297	    rw [List.ofFn_succ]
1298	    apply isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring h1
1299	    have hmapeq : (List.ofFn (fun i : Fin m => rs i.succ)).map
1300	        (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
1301	        = List.ofFn (fun i => Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) (rs i.succ)) := by
1302	      rw [List.map_ofFn]; rfl
1303	    rw [hmapeq]; exact hih

1305	/-- **Stacks `00TT`, Jacobian-criterion direction (regularity conclusion).**
1306	For a smooth integral variety `X` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, the
1307	stalk of `X` at every point is a regular local ring.
1308	
1309	This is the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00TT` (Lemma 10.140.3
1310	"Smooth algebras over fields"): given an affine chart `Spec A ⊆ X`, the
1311	\(\bar k\)-algebra `A` is smooth at every prime `𝔮`, and the localisation
1312	`A_𝔮 = X.presheaf.stalk z` (for `z ∈ X` lying over `𝔮`) is regular by the
1313	"in this case the local ring `A_𝔮` is regular" clause of the cited lemma.
1314

1600	/-- **Smooth + codim-1 ⇒ DVR.** For a nonsingular integral variety `X` over
1601	an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with
1602	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
1603	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).
1604	
1605	The proof reduces to "regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` is a DVR"
1606	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
1607	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
1608	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
1609	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the
1610	DVR instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.
1611	
1612	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
1613	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/
1614	theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
1615	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1616	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1617	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1618	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1619	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1620	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
1621	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1622	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
1623	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
1624	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
1625	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
1626	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
1627	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
1628	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
1629	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
1630	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
1631	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
1632	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
1633	  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
1634	  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
1635	  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
1636	  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
1637	  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
1638	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1639	    intro hF
1640	    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq).mp hF)
1641	  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
1642	  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.
1643	  exact ((tfae.out 0 4).mpr hprin)

1647	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
1648	
1649	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
1650	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
1651	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
1652	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
1653	
1654	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
1655	
1656	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
1657	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
1658	
1659	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
1660	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1661	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
1662	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
1663	
1664	The proof combines two steps:
1665	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
1666	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
1667	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
1668	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
1669	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
1670	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
1671	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
1672	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
1673	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
1674	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
1675	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
1676	
1677	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1678	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
1679	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
1680	
1681	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
1682	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
1683	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
1684	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1685	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1686	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1687	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1688	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1689	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1690	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
1691	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1692	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1693	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
1694	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1695	  -- Derived structural instance: the target Y.left is a separated scheme
1696	  -- (as `Y.hom : Y.left ⟶ Spec kbar` is separated and `Spec kbar` is affine
1697	  -- hence separated over the terminal).
1698	  haveI : Y.left.IsSeparated := by
1699	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1700	    have heq : terminal.from Y.left = Y.hom ≫ terminal.from _ := Subsingleton.elim _ _
1701	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
1702	  -- The substantive Milne 3.1 proof body reduces to showing Dom(f) = ⊤:
1703	  --
1704	  -- * Step 1 (codim-1 components of Z(f) ruled out) uses the valuative
1705	  --   criterion of properness on the complete target Y plus the DVR-stalk
1706	  --   structure at codim-1 points (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` above, gated
1707	  --   on `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6 Mathlib gap).
1708	  -- * Step 2 (codim-≥2 extension) uses the depth-≥2 local-cohomology
1709	  --   vanishing `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point (Stacks 0AVF), a
1710	  --   genuine Mathlib gap at commit `b80f227`. The codim-1-free hypothesis
1711	  --   `_hf` alone suffices for Step 1's conclusion but Step 2 requires the
1712	  --   local-cohomology / `Module.depth` bridge from
1713	  --   `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`).
1714	  --
1715	  -- Once `Dom(f) = ⊤` is established, the unique morphism extension is the
1716	  -- composition `X.left.topIso.inv ≫ (X.left.isoOfEq h).inv ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom`;
1717	  -- uniqueness follows from the reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1718	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` / `PartialMap.equiv_iff_of_isSeparated`).
1719	  --
1720	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: this lane is gated on Stacks 0AVF
1721	  -- (depth-≥2 H¹ vanishing) + `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6.
1722	  sorry

1724	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1725	
1726	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1727	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1728	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
1729	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
1730	
1731	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1732	
1733	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1734	codim 1.**
1735	
1736	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1737	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1738	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1739	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
1740	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
1741	
1742	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1743	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1744	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1745	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1746	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1747	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1748	
1749	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1750	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1751	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1752	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1753	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1754	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1755	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1756	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1757	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1758	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1759	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1760	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1761	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
1762	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1763	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1764	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1765	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
1766	  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
1767	  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
1768	  -- (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1) is the
1769	  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
1770	  --
1771	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1772	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
1773	  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
1774	  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
1775	  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
1776	  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
1777	  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1778	  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
1779	  --   the domain of definition.
1780	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1781	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1782	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1783	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1784	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1785	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1786	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1787	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1788	  --   This is the substantive Mathlib gap: Mathlib's

1081	\section{Milne Theorem 3.1: codimension-\(\geq 2\) extension}
1082	\label{sec:milne_thm31}
1083	
1084	The first input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is the following: a rational map from a normal
1085	variety to a \emph{complete} variety automatically extends across every codim-\(1\)
1086	point.
1087	
1088	\begin{theorem}
1089	
1090	\leanok
1091	  [Milne Theorem~3.1: codim-\(\geq 2\) extension to a complete target]
1092	  \label{thm:codim_one_extension}
1093	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth}
1094	  
1095	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16
1096	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
1097	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textsc{Theorem 3.1.} \emph{A rational map $\varphi \colon V
1098	  % \dashrightarrow W\( from a normal variety \)V\( to a complete variety \)W$ is defined
1099	  % on an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension
1100	  % \(\geq 2\).}"
1101	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16.}
1102	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(Y\) be a complete variety over
1103	  \(\bar k\), and let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. Then the
1104	  indeterminacy locus \(Z(f)\) (\cref{def:indeterminacy_locus}) has codimension
1105	  \(\geq 2\) in \(X\); equivalently, \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
1106	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}).
1107	
1108	  Specialising further: if in addition \(f\) \emph{is} codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
1109	  ``for free'' (i.e.\ no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in \(Z(f)\)), then
1110	  \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) = X\) and \(f\) extends to a regular morphism $\widetilde f \colon X
1111	  \to Y\(. The extension is unique because \)X\( is reduced and \)\mathrm{Dom}(f)$ is dense
1112	  in \(X\).
1113	\end{theorem}
1114	
1115	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "\textsc{Proof.} Assume first that \(V\) is a curve. Thus we are
1116	% given a nonsingular curve \(V\) and a regular map \(\varphi \colon U \to W\) from an
1117	% open subset of \(V\) which we want to extend to \(V\). Consider the maps
1118	% [diagram: \(U \hookrightarrow V\), $U \xrightarrow{u \mapsto (u, \varphi(u))} Z
1119	% \subseteq V \times W\(, \)U \xrightarrow{\varphi} W$, with projections from
1120	% \(V \times W\) to \(V\) and to \(W\)]
1121	% Let \(U'\) be the image of \(U\) in \(V \times W\), and let \(Z\) be its closure. The image
1122	% of \(Z\) in \(V\) is closed (because \(W\) is complete), and contains \(U\) (the composite
1123	% \(U \to V\) is the given inclusion), and so \(Z\) maps onto \(V\). The maps $U \to U'
1124	% \to U\( are isomorphisms. Therefore, \)Z \to V$ is a surjective map from a complete
1125	% curve onto a nonsingular complete curve that is an isomorphism on open subsets.
1126	% Such a map must be an isomorphism (complete nonsingular curves are determined by
1127	% their function fields). The restriction of the projection map \(V \times W \to W\)
1128	% to \(Z\ (\simeq V)\) is the extension of \(\varphi\) to \(V\) we are seeking.
1129	%   The general case can be reduced to the one-dimensional case (using schemes). Let
1130	% \(U\) be the largest subset on which \(\varphi\) is defined, and suppose that $V
1131	% \smallsetminus U\( has codimension 1. Then there is a prime divisor \)Z\( in \)V
1132	% \smallsetminus U\(. Because \)V$ is normal, its associated local ring is a discrete
1133	% valuation ring \(\mathcal O_Z\) with field of fractions \(k(V)\). The map \(\varphi\)
1134	% defines a morphism of schemes \(\operatorname{Spec}(k(V)) \to W\), which the
1135	% valuative criterion of properness (Hartshorne 1977, II 4.7) shows extends to a
1136	% morphism \(\operatorname{Spec}(\mathcal O_Z) \to W\). This implies that \(\varphi\)
1137	% has a representative defined on an open subset that meets \(Z\) in a nonempty set,
1138	% which is a contradiction."
1139	\begin{proof}
1140	  
1141	  Following Milne's two-step proof.
1142	
1143	  \emph{Step 1: rule out codim-\(1\) components.} Suppose for contradiction that \(Z(f)\)
1144	  contains a prime divisor \(W \subseteq X\) (i.e.\ a codim-\(1\) irreducible component).
1145	  Let \(\eta \in W\) be its generic point, so \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\). By
1146	  \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a discrete
1147	  valuation ring with fraction field the function field \(K(X)\). Any representative
1148	  \((U, \varphi_U) \in f\) has \(U \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f) \neq \emptyset\) and so determines
1149	  a \(\bar k\)-algebra map from the function field \(K(Y)\) of \(Y\) to \(K(X)\): explicitly,
1150	  \(\varphi_U\) defines a morphism of schemes \(\operatorname{Spec}(K(X)) \to Y\) (since
1151	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(K(X))\) is the generic point of \(X\)). By the valuative criterion
1152	  of properness applied to the complete variety \(Y\)
1153	  (\emph{cf.}\ Hartshorne~II.4.7, cited verbatim in Milne~3.1's proof), this morphism
1154	  extends to a morphism \(\operatorname{Spec}(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}) \to Y\). Standard
1155	  algebraic-geometry spreading then exhibits an open neighbourhood \(V\) of \(\eta\) in
1156	  \(X\) on which \(f\) has a regular representative; in particular $\eta \in
1157	  \mathrm{Dom}(f)\(, contradicting \)\eta \in W \subseteq Z(f)\(. Hence no codim-\)1$
1158	  component of \(X\) lies in \(Z(f)\), i.e.\ \(Z(f)\) has codimension \(\geq 2\).
1159	
1160	  \emph{Step 2: extend across codim-\(\geq 2\) points (the "codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1161	  \(\Rightarrow\) extension" half).} Suppose now that \(Z(f)\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)
1162	  but is non-empty (we want to deduce \(Z(f) = \emptyset\) under the additional input
1163	  that ``\(f\) is already codim-1-indeterminacy-free, by hypothesis''). Pick a closed
1164	  point \(x \in Z(f)\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{x\}} \geq 2\). By
1165	  \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} applied to the regular local ring
1166	  \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\), we have
1167	  \(\mathrm{depth}(\mathcal O_{X, x}) = \dim(\mathcal O_{X, x}) \geq 2\). The sheaf
1168	  \(\mathcal O_X\) on the punctured neighbourhood \(V \smallsetminus \{x\}\)
1169	  (any small affine neighbourhood \(V \ni x\)) has \(H^0_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0\)
1170	  and \(H^1_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0\) (the depth-\(\geq 2\) characterisation of
1171	  local-cohomology vanishing, Stacks tag \texttt{0AVF}; cf.\ Hartshorne~III.3.5 for
1172	  the surface case). Pull back the coordinates of any affine chart of \(Y\) around the
1173	  closure \(\overline{f(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \cap V)}\) along \(f\); the resulting sections of
1174	  \(\mathcal O_X\) on \(V \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) extend uniquely to sections on \(V\),
1175	  yielding a regular representative of \(f\) on \(V\). Hence \(x \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\),
1176	  contradicting \(x \in Z(f)\). Therefore \(Z(f) = \emptyset\) and \(f\) extends to a
1177	  regular morphism \(\widetilde f \colon X \to Y\).
1178	
1179	  Uniqueness of \(\widetilde f\) is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement
1180	  principle: two morphisms \(X \to Y\) that agree on the dense open \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\)
1181	  agree on all of \(X\).
1182	\end{proof}
1183	
1184	\begin{remark}
1185	  \label{rmk:codim1_role_of_ab}
1186	  \textbf{Where Auslander--Buchsbaum enters.} Step~1 of the proof uses only normality
1187	  + the valuative criterion of properness (Hartshorne~II.4.7) and is characteristic-free.
1188	  Step~2 uses the depth-\(\geq 2\) extension property at a codim-\(\geq 2\) regular
1189	  point, which is exactly what \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} of
1190	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum} supplies (regular local of dimension \(d\)
1191	  \(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay of depth \(d\)). For our application the source \(X\) is a
1192	  smooth variety, so the regular-local-ring hypothesis is automatic; the Cohen--Macaulay
1193	  consequence is what feeds the local-cohomology vanishing.
1194	
1195	  Concretely, when \(X\) is a smooth surface (e.g.\ \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1\) or
1196	  \(C \times C\) for a smooth projective curve \(C\)), the codim-\(\geq 2\) closed points of
1197	  \(X\) are exactly the closed points of \(X\) (because \(\dim X = 2\)), and at each such
1198	  point \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\) is regular of dimension \(2\), hence Cohen--Macaulay of
1199	  depth \(2\). The smooth-surface case is the one A.4.c will invoke when extending the
1200	  symmetric-product rational map \(C^{(g)} \dashrightarrow A\) along the Abel--Jacobi
1201	  birational equivalence to \(J = \Pic^0_{C/k}\).
1202	\end{remark}

1204	\section{Milne Lemma 3.3: indeterminacy locus of a map to a group variety is codim 1}
1205	\label{sec:milne_lem33}
1206	
1207	The second input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is structural: when the target \(Y\) is a group
1208	variety, the indeterminacy locus is either empty or of \emph{pure} codimension \(1\).
1209	Combined with \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s ``codimension \(\geq 2\)'' conclusion,
1210	this forces the locus to be empty.
1211	
1212	\begin{lemma}
1213	
1214	\leanok
1215	  [Milne Lemma~3.3: pure-codim-\(1\) structure of the indeterminacy locus into a group
1216	  variety]
1217	  \label{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}
1218	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme}
1219	  
1220	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Lemma~3.3, \S I.3, p.~17
1221	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 23).
1222	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textsc{Lemma 3.3.} \emph{Let \(\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow G\)
1223	  % be a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety. Then either
1224	  % \(\varphi\) is defined on all of \(V\) or the points where it is not defined form a
1225	  % closed subset of pure codimension 1 in \(V\) (i.e., a finite union of prime
1226	  % divisors).}"
1227	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Lemma~3.3, \S I.3, p.~17.}
1228	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(G\) be a group variety over
1229	  \(\bar k\) (a smooth group scheme of finite type), and let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow
1230	  G\( be a rational map. Then either \)f\( is defined on all of \)X$, or the indeterminacy
1231	  locus \(Z(f) \subseteq X\) is a closed subset of pure codimension \(1\) (i.e.\ a finite
1232	  union of prime divisors).
1233	
1234	  Equivalently: it is \emph{never} the case that \(f\) has a non-empty indeterminacy
1235	  locus all of whose irreducible components have codimension \(\geq 2\).
1236	\end{lemma}

1264	%   $\mathcal O_{V \times V, (x, x)} = \{f \in k(V \times V) \mid
1265	%   \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty \text{ does not contain } (x, x)\} \cup \{0\}$.
1266	% Suppose \(\phi\) is not defined at \(x\). Then for some $f \in \operatorname{Im}
1267	% (\varphi^\ast)\(, \)(x, x) \in \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty\(, and clearly \)\Phi$ is
1268	% not defined at the points \((y, y) \in \Delta \cap \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty\).
1269	% This is a subset of pure codimension one in \(\Delta\) (AG 9.2), and when we identify
1270	% it with a subset of \(V\), it is a subset of \(V\) of codimension one passing through
1271	% \(x\) on which \(\varphi\) is not defined."
1272	\begin{proof}
1273	  
1274	  Following Milne's proof of Lemma~3.3. The argument has four sub-steps. Throughout we
1275	  fix a representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\).
1276	
1277	  \emph{Sub-step 1: the difference rational map.} Define
1278	  \[
1279	    \Phi \colon X \times X \dashrightarrow G,
1280	    \qquad
1281	    \Phi(x, y) \;:=\; \varphi(x) \cdot \varphi(y)^{-1},
1282	  \]
1283	  via the composition
1284	  \[
1285	    U \times U
1286	    \;\xrightarrow{\varphi_U \times \varphi_U}\; G \times G
1287	    \;\xrightarrow{\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv}}\; G \times G
1288	    \;\xrightarrow{m}\; G,
1289	  \]
1290	  where \(\mathrm{inv} \colon G \to G\) is the group-variety inverse and
1291	  \(m \colon G \times G \to G\) is the group law. The map \(\Phi\) is rational by
1292	  composition: its domain is at least \(U \times U \subseteq X \times X\), which is dense
1293	  because \(U\) is dense in \(X\).
1294	
1295	  \emph{Sub-step 2: \(\Phi\)-defined at \((x, x)\) \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\varphi\)-defined at
1296	  \(x\).} If \(\varphi\) is defined at \(x\), the explicit formula \(\Phi(x, x) = e\) (the
1297	  identity of \(G\)) shows \(\Phi\) is defined at \((x, x)\). Conversely, if \(\Phi\) is
1298	  defined at \((x, x)\), openness of \(\mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)\) in \(X \times X\) produces an
1299	  open set \(W \ni (x, x)\) on which \(\Phi\) is defined; by projection (taking the slice
1300	  \(\{x\} \times W' \subseteq W\) for some open \(W' \subseteq X\), possibly shrinking
1301	  \(W'\) so \(\varphi\) is defined on \(W'\) as well --- \(W'\) need not contain \(x\)), the
1302	  identity
1303	  \[
1304	    \varphi(x) \;=\; \Phi(x, u) \cdot \varphi(u),
1305	    \qquad u \in W',
1306	  \]
1307	  exhibits a regular representative of \(\varphi\) at \(x\). Hence $x \in
1308	  \mathrm{Dom}(\varphi)\( if and only if \)(x, x) \in \mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)$.
1309	
1310	  \emph{Sub-step 3: rephrase via pullback of local rings.} The rational map \(\Phi\)
1311	  pulls \(\mathcal O_{G, e}\) back to a \(\bar k\)-subalgebra of the function field
1312	  \(K(X \times X)\):
1313	  \[
1314	    \Phi^\ast \colon \mathcal O_{G, e} \;\longrightarrow\; K(X \times X).
1315	  \]
1316	  Because \(\Phi\) sends \((x, x)\) to \(e\) wherever it is defined, \(\Phi\) is defined at
1317	  \((x, x)\) if and only if $\Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e}) \subseteq \mathcal O_{X
1318	  \times X, (x, x)}\( (i.e.\ every local function on \)G\( near \)e$ has no pole at
1319	  \((x, x)\) after pullback).
1320	
1321	  \emph{Sub-step 4: pole locus is pure codimension \(1\).} The variety \(X \times X\) is
1322	  nonsingular (smoothness is stable under products over \(\bar k\)), so the Weil-divisor
1323	  apparatus of \cref{def:codim1_cycles} and the order map of
1324	  \cref{def:order_at_point} apply on \(X \times X\). For any nonzero rational function
1325	  \(f \in K(X \times X)^\ast\) decompose its divisor as
1326	  \[
1327	    \mathrm{div}(f) \;=\; \mathrm{div}(f)_0 - \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty,
1328	  \]
1329	  with \(\mathrm{div}(f)_0\) and \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) effective and
1330	  \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty = \mathrm{div}(f^{-1})_0\) (Milne's notation, matching the
1331	  project's \(\div(f) = \sum \ord_Y(f) \cdot Y\) of
1332	  \cref{def:principal_divisor}: \(\mathrm{div}(f)_0\) collects the prime divisors with
1333	  \(\ord_Y(f) > 0\) and \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) those with \(\ord_Y(f) < 0\)). The local
1334	  ring at \((x, x)\) can be characterised as
1335	  \[
1336	    \mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}
1337	    \;=\; \bigl\{\, f \in K(X \times X) \;\big|\;
1338	    \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty \text{ does not contain } (x, x) \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
1339	  \]
1340	  Suppose \(f \in K(X \times X)^\ast\) is not in \(\mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}\);
1341	  equivalently, \((x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty)\). The pole divisor
1342	  \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) is a finite sum of prime divisors of \(X \times X\) (i.e.\ a
1343	  closed subset of pure codimension \(1\) in \(X \times X\)). Its intersection with the
1344	  diagonal \(\Delta_X \subseteq X \times X\) is a closed subset of pure codimension \(1\)
1345	  in \(\Delta_X\) (this is Milne's reference to AG~9.2, the standard fact that a prime
1346	  divisor of \(X \times X\) either contains \(\Delta_X\) or meets it in pure codimension
1347	  \(1\); on a nonsingular variety this is an instance of Krull's principal-ideal theorem
1348	  applied to the local equation of \(\Delta_X\) inside \(X \times X\)).
1349	
1350	  \emph{Conclusion.} Suppose \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow G\) is not defined on all of
1351	  \(X\). Pick \(x \in Z(f)\). By Sub-steps~2--3, there is some $f_0 \in
1352	  \Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e})\( with \)(x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty)$.
1353	  By Sub-step~4 the intersection $\mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty) \cap
1354	  \Delta_X\( is pure codimension \)1\( in \)\Delta_X\(, passing through \)(x, x)$.
1355	  Identifying \(\Delta_X\) with \(X\) via the diagonal isomorphism, the corresponding
1356	  closed subset of \(X\) has pure codimension \(1\) and passes through \(x\). By Sub-step~2,
1357	  every point of this codim-\(1\) subset is a point where \(\Phi\) is undefined on the
1358	  diagonal, hence where \(\varphi\) is undefined. Thus \(Z(\varphi)\) contains a pure
1359	  codim-\(1\) closed subset passing through \(x\); since \(x \in Z(\varphi)\) was arbitrary,
1360	  every irreducible component of \(Z(\varphi)\) has codimension exactly \(1\), i.e.\
1361	  \(Z(\varphi)\) is of pure codimension \(1\).
1362	\end{proof}
1363	
1364	\begin{remark}
1365	  \label{rmk:milne_lem33_charfree}
1366	  \textbf{Characteristic-free.} The above proof uses only: (i) the group law on \(G\)
1367	  and its inverse (formal smoothness of \(G\) as a group object); (ii) the Weil-divisor
1368	  apparatus on the nonsingular variety \(X \times X\) (Hartshorne~II.6, or
1369	  \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor} in the project's notation); (iii) the
1370	  decomposition of a divisor on a nonsingular variety into effective pieces, and
1371	  Krull's principal-ideal theorem on \(X \times X\). None of these has a characteristic
1372	  restriction, so \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} holds over every
1373	  algebraically closed base field, in arbitrary characteristic. (Milne states the
1374	  lemma in this generality at \S I.3, p.~17.)
1375	\end{remark}

1377	\section{Weil-divisor obstruction to codim-1 extension}
1378	\label{sec:weil_obstruction}
1379	
1380	The proof of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s Step~1 (codim-\(1\) ruling-out) goes
1381	through the valuative criterion. In the project's Weil-divisor language
1382	(\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}) the same obstruction has a cleaner phrasing:
1383	the extension fails at a prime divisor \(W\) if and only if some affine coordinate of
1384	\(f\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\) has a strictly negative order
1385	\(\ord_W(\cdot) < 0\). This is the form the Lean prover needs when consuming
1386	\cref{def:order_at_point} and \cref{def:principal_divisor}.
1387	
1388	\begin{theorem}
1389	
1390	  [Weil-divisor characterisation of the codim-1 extension obstruction]
1391	  \label{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
1392	  
1393	  % NOTE (iter-179 review, lvb-checker `codimone-iter179` corrective):
1394	  % `\lean{...}` pin DETACHED (Path D2 option (b) per the lvb-checker). The
1395	  % substantive Hartshorne-II.6 valuative reformulation (the iff with
1396	  % ord_W >= 0) below is the *intended* statement; the iter-179 Lane D
1397	  % `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` decl is only a 2-LOC
1398	  % `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` reshuffle that does NOT prove the
1399	  % ord_W content, so re-pinning `\lean{...}` to it would falsely claim
1400	  % kernel-clean formalization of the substantive Hartshorne iff. The
1401	  % weaker reshuffle proven this iter will be pinned to its own light
1402	  % `\begin{lemma}` block (working title `lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle`)
1403	  % by an iter-180+ blueprint-writer dispatch; the substantive Hartshorne
1404	  % version stays pinned here but unlinked until a future

1406	  % `Scheme.RationalMap.order` machinery (pullback `RationalMap →

1409	  % proof-journal/sessions/session_179/recommendations.md REC-14.

1413	  % structural, not a \lean{...} typo: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6
1414	  % ord_W >= 0 reformulation requires the
1415	  % Scheme.RationalMap-to-function-field pullback machinery, which is
1416	  % not in Mathlib at commit b80f227 and is not yet built in this
1417	  % project. The weaker iter-179 reshuffle Lean declaration
1418	  % mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point is blueprint-pinned
1419	  % at \cref{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle} as the honest
1420	  % fallback; thm:weil_divisor_obstruction here remains unpinned at the
1421	  % \lean{...} level until the genuine pullback decl lands.)
1422	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, pp.~130--131 (the valuation \(v_Y\) associated to a
1423	  % prime divisor, and the characterisation of \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_Y}\) as the
1424	  % subring of \(K(X)\) on which \(v_Y \geq 0\)) (read from
1425	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages 147--148).
1426	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If \(Y\) is a prime divisor on \(X\), let \(\eta \in Y\) be its generic
1427	  % point. Then the local ring \(\mathcal O_{\eta, X}\) of \(X\) at \(\eta\) is a discrete
1428	  % valuation ring with quotient field \(K\), the function field of \(X\). We call the
1429	  % corresponding discrete valuation \(v_Y\) the \emph{valuation of \(Y\)}."
1430	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Now let \(f \in K^\ast\) be any nonzero rational function on \(X\).
1431	  % Then \(v_Y(f)\) is an integer. If it is positive, we say \(f\) has a \emph{zero} along
1432	  % \(Y\), of that order; if it is negative, we say \(f\) has a \emph{pole} along \(Y\), of
1433	  % order \(-v_Y(f)\)."
1434	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, pp.~130--131 (valuation \(v_Y\) and the order map).}
1435	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(Y\) be a variety over \(\bar k\),
1436	  let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map, and let \(W \subseteq X\) be a
1437	  prime divisor with generic point \(\eta_W \in W\) (so
1438	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta_W\}} = 1\)). Let \((U, \varphi_U)\) be any representative of
1439	  \(f\) with \(U \cap (X \smallsetminus W) \neq \emptyset\). The following are equivalent:
1440	  \begin{enumerate}
1441	    \item \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) (i.e.\ \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\), equivalently
1442	      \(W \not\subseteq Z(f)\)).
1443	    \item For some (equivalently, every) affine open neighbourhood \(V \subseteq Y\) of
1444	      the generic image of \(f\) near \(W\), every regular function $g \in
1445	      \mathcal O_Y(V)\( pulls back to a rational function \)\varphi_U^\ast(g) \in K(X)$
1446	      satisfying
1447	      \[
1448	        \ord_W\bigl(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\bigr) \;\geq\; 0.
1449	      \]
1450	  \end{enumerate}
1451	
1452	  Consequently: \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
1453	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}) if and only if, for every prime divisor
1454	  \(W \subseteq X\) and every affine open \(V \subseteq Y\) as above, the pullback
1455	  \(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\) has \(\ord_W \geq 0\) for all \(g \in \mathcal O_Y(V)\).
1456	\end{theorem}
1457	
1458	\begin{proof}
1459	  
1460	  By \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\) is a
1461	  DVR with fraction field \(K(X)\) and uniformiser determined by \(W\); by
1462	  \cref{def:order_at_point} its valuation is exactly \(\ord_W\). The local ring at
1463	  \(\eta_W\) is therefore characterised inside \(K(X)\) as the subring
1464	  \[
1465	    \mathcal O_{X, \eta_W} \;=\; \bigl\{\, h \in K(X) \;\big|\; \ord_W(h) \geq 0
1466	    \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
1467	  \]
1468	  The rational map \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) if and only if, for any affine open
1469	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec} A \subseteq Y\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\),
1470	  the pullback \(\varphi_U^\ast \colon A \to K(X)\) lands in
1471	  \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\): this is the standard local characterisation of where a
1472	  rational map is regular at a codim-\(1\) point of a smooth variety (it is the
1473	  ``regular = no pole'' criterion). By the displayed identity, the latter is
1474	  equivalent to \(\ord_W(\varphi_U^\ast(g)) \geq 0\) for every \(g \in A\), which is
1475	  condition (2). The equivalence (1)~\(\Leftrightarrow\)~(2) is therefore established.
1476	
1477	  For the consequence, \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free iff no prime divisor \(W\) is
1478	  contained in \(Z(f)\), which by the equivalence above is iff the \(\ord_W \geq 0\)
1479	  condition holds for every prime divisor \(W\).
1480	\end{proof}
1481	
1482	\begin{remark}
1483	  \label{rmk:weil_obstruction_application}
1484	  \textbf{Why this form is the prover-facing one.} In the consumer chapter
1485	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} the Lean prover combines
1486	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} with \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}; in
1487	  isolation, the abstract codim-\(\geq 2\) statement
1488	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} suffices and \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
1489	  is not invoked. The Weil-divisor form is the bridge to the project's
1490	  \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API, which is the form A.4.a's own internal sub-proofs
1491	  use when establishing \emph{specific} extensions in genus-\(0\) classifying-map
1492	  arguments and in the symmetric-product Albanese chapter
1493	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AlbaneseUP}.
1494	\end{remark}

1496	\begin{lemma}
1497	
1498	\leanok
1499	  [Definedness at a prime divisor, repackaged as a through-the-point partial map]
1500	  \label{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle}
1501	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point}
1502	  Let \(X\) and \(Y\) be varieties over \(\bar k\) and let
1503	  \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. For a prime divisor
1504	  \(W \subseteq X\) with generic point \(\eta_W = W.\mathrm{point}\), the
1505	  following are equivalent:
1506	  \begin{enumerate}
1507	    \item \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) (\(f\) is defined at the generic point of
1508	      \(W\)).
1509	    \item There exists a partial-map representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\) ---
1510	      i.e.\ a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) on a dense open
1511	      \(U \subseteq X\) whose induced rational map equals \(f\) --- whose domain
1512	      \(U\) contains \(\eta_W\).
1513	  \end{enumerate}
1514	\end{lemma}
1515	
1516	\begin{proof}
1517	
1518	  The lemma is a definitional reshuffle of the Mathlib membership predicate
1519	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.mem\_domain}, which already states that
1520	  \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) iff there is some partial-map representative of
1521	  \(f\) defined at \(\eta_W\); the only content here is reordering the two
1522	  conjuncts under the existential so the \(\varphi_U \mapsto f\) component lands
1523	  first (matching the way later witnesses in the project construct partial-map
1524	  representatives). The forward and backward implications are immediate from
1525	  the unfolded definition.
1526	\end{proof}
1527	
1528	\begin{remark}
1529	  \label{rmk:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle_scope}

1531	  \cref{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle} is the iter-179 Lane~D honest
1532	  fallback for the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 ``regular = no pole''
1533	  reformulation that \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} states in its full
1534	  \(\ord_W \geq 0\) form. The reshuffle captures only the
1535	  definitional half of the equivalence (existence of a partial-map
1536	  representative through a prime-divisor generic point); upgrading it to the
1537	  substantive \(\ord_W \geq 0\) form requires the
1538	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap}-to-function-field pullback machinery, which is
1539	  not shipped by Mathlib at commit \texttt{b80f227}. See the iter-179
1540	  \texttt{\% NOTE} block on \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} for the
1541	  history.
